differential_diagnoses: Amyloidosis (systemic, specifically AL or ATTR with cardiac and bulbar involvement)
Metabolic myopathy (e.g., acid maltase deficiency/Pompe disease)
next_steps: Perform serum acetylcholine receptor antibody (AChR) testing and muscle-specific kinase (MuSK) antibody testing
Conduct electromyography (EMG) and repetitive nerve stimulation study
Evaluate for systemic amyloidosis with serum/urine protein electrophoresis with immunofixation and free light chain assay
Obtain cardiology consultation for cardiac MRI and consideration of endomyocardial biopsy if infiltrative cardiomyopathy is suspected
Monitor for impending respiratory failure and maintain low threshold for intubation
Consider edrophonium or neostigmine challenge test if myasthenia gravis is strongly suspected
Perform lumbar puncture for cerebrospinal fluid analysis (protein level and cytology)
